name: hostapd-mana

documentation.readme: A featureful rogue access point first presented at Defcon 22 by Dominic White (@singe) & Ian de Villiers @ sensepost (research@sensepost.com)

arguments.name: --no-wait
arguments.description: do not wait for tag to be removed before exiting

arguments.name: --only-one
arguments.description: run only one operation and exit

arguments.name: --success
arguments.description: success file for writing success update

arguments.name: --summary
arguments.description: summary file for writing status updates

documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `hostapd-mana` from its upstream repository, then run `hostapd-mana --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags.
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: hostapd-mana
============.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A featureful rogue access point first presented at Defcon 22 by Dominic White (@singe) & Ian de Villiers @ sensepost (research@sensepost.com).

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need Wireless, use hostapd-mana because hostapd-mana
============.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair hostapd-mana with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `hostapd-mana --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
